Question: Solve the triangle shown in Figure 8 to the nearest tenth. Figure 8
Given $$\alpha = 80° , a = 120 ,$$ and $$b = 121 ,$$ find the missing side and angles. If there is more than one possible solution, show both.
Answer: Solution 1 $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}\alpha = 80° & a = 120 \\ \beta \approx 83.2° & b = 121 \\ \gamma \approx 16.8° & c \approx 35.2\end{matrix}\right .$$ Solution 2 $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}\alpha^{\prime} = 80° a^{\prime} = 120 \\ \beta^{\prime} \approx 96.8° b^{\prime} = 121 \\ \gamma^{\prime} \approx 3.2° c^{\prime} \approx 6.8\end{matrix}\right .$$

Question: Given $$\alpha = 80° , a = 100 , b = 10 ,$$ find the missing side and angles. If there is more than one possible solution, show both. Round your answers to the nearest tenth.
Determine the number of triangles possible given $$a = 31 ,$$ $$b = 26 ,$$ $$\beta = 48° .$$
Find the area of the triangle given $$\beta = 42° ,$$ $$a = 7.2 \text{ft} ,$$ $$c = 3.4 \text{ft} .$$ Round the area to the nearest tenth.

Find the height of the blimp if the angle of elevation at the southern end zone, point A, is 70°, the angle of elevation from the northern end zone, point $$B ,$$ is 62°, and the distance between the viewing points of the two end zones is 145 yards.
